security.content: Footprint’s privacy-preserving technology lets you satisfy KYC without needing to ever touch PII. Our secure PII vault protects your user's data while saving you time and money.
security.title: Best-in-class security

accuracy.content: Footprint leverages cutting-edge biometric scans, liveness checks, and peer-to-peer verification to validate real people in real time. Our ecosystem keeps bad actors out while creating a seamless experience for real users.
accuracy.title: Unprecedented accuracy

cheaper.content: Footprint’s technology combines storage and verification. Our solution is up to 2x cheaper than traditional methods to verify and store PII because we are able to condense what today requires multiple tools into one.
cheaper.title: More functionality, less dollars

vault.title: Own the data, disown the risk.
vault.subtitle: PII vault
vault.description: Our advanced PII vaults are built on top of secure Nitro Enclaves. Your customer's data is end-to-end encrypted: it can only be processed and read within our secure environment.

main.title: Privacy and security at your engineering team's fingertips
main.content: Instead of building or buying your own data vaulting system, use Footprint's integrated vaulting to achieve strong cryptographic protection for your most sensitive data.

trust.title: Zero-trust by design
trust.content: Our permissioned API ensures only the right parties can access the data they're allowed to see. PII data is never stored in plaintext.

access.title: Granular data access
access.content: Limit the exposure, only access the bare minimum data you need. Nothing else leaves our enclave-backed vaults.

secure.title: Secure enclave backed
secure.content: Our system guarantees that all PII data is always encrypted to our network gapped, storage / cpu / memory isolated Nitro Enclaves.

what-is-kyc.title: What is KYC?
content: Know Your Customer (KYC)

satisfy-kyc.title: Does this satisfy KYC?
content: Yes, and then some. Our system is designed to comply with Section 326 of the Patriot Act (KYC)and the Safe Banking Act. You, as our customer, still own the data of your users, but we simply store it (no different from AWS hosting you in their cloud). You at all times can call from our server a token unique to each user with an audit log of how we verified their identity (this updates in real-time, as we continuously increase our accuracy based on how users interact with other applications), and both you and your banking partner can request access to specific PII attributes. Footprint has had conversations with Directors at FinCEN and the top legal minds in the field to ensure as much.

too-good.title: This seems too good to be true—you handle KYC + PII Vaulting—what’s the catch?
content: The catch was that you were paying for those services separately before :). We aren’t geniuses—it is actually simpler, quicker, and more secure to do both of these in one solution. By virtue of us securely storing data, we verify it (for example, by storing John Doe’s encrypted SSN as credentialed by the Social Security Administration, we are in real-time verifying John Doe’s identity in conjunction with our liveness checks).

We think it is silly that in the past, companies have had to pay to tokenize/vault this KYC data that was of little use to them, and never evolved. Fraudsters are always changing their approach, but nothing screams static like tokenizing KYC data upon account creation. Our vault with PII is a living mechanism. We constantly update our predictions on people’s identity based on new signals. For companies, this means they benefit from future KYCs done on their users identities.

customize-kyc.title: Other places let me customize KYC. Why don’t you?
content: We don’t think KYC should be customized, and it certainly should not be a toggle of accuracy. There are certain attributes required by law that need to be collected (SSN, DOB), and then attributes that help us better confirm your identity (phone + email, and how long you’ve had each of those). We get all of this data and check it from the best data sources to remove complexity. Truth be told, this is why we think KYC is becoming commoditized, and why our product is built around security/storage of the data, real-time fraud monitoring, and the best developer experience. Our competitors don’t do any of those things, so they add complexity to their own product (you definitely don’t need 82 data vendors to choose from) or fancy “magic” (wow—that selfie check on your driver’s license is so cool ;)) to obfuscate the truth. We solve KYC for you, and just give you the six lines of code to do it. We know you’re busy building an amazing product, so take care of KYC for you.

internet-fraud.title: Is Internet fraud that big of a problem?
content: There is $50B of identity theft a year despite enterprises spending $20B on IDV. The problem is only getting worse; an IDology study found a 53% increase in fraud attempts in 2020. Enterprises report 15%-50% account abandonment when people must go through a ~5 minute document verification. Meanwhile, even large Neobanks with $1M budgets say they are still only accurate 98.5% of the time; with 1M+ verifications a year this means they miss 1500 people a year with the cost of a bad actor ranging from $300-$30k. And this accuracy is much lower for growing fintechs with much fewer resources. Identity shouldn’t be a toggle; it should be 100% accurate. And it needs to be in the emerging world of embedded fintech.

why-vaulting.title: We built an in-house solution to store the data, so we really just need help with KYC. Why do we need your vaulting?
content: This is a common question we get, often because historically these have been seen as different functions performed by separate teams. As such, it may not be immediately clear how risk teams can solve issues for security team, and vice versa. While in-house solutions for vaulting can be a good starting point, there are many problems with them over time. It is expensive to maintain these structures—whether it be cost of architecture or opportunity cost of the hours your own engineering teams are being consumed keeping the vaults secure. Further, even if you build the vault, you will need to likely still pay for tools like BigID and OneTrust to label the fact that you securely stored the PII.

Even if you adapted our nitro enclaves and brought advanced encryption to your environments, it would be massively disruptive to generate the zero-trust environment we have built by default within Footprint. For example, some investment apps need to access an SSN for trades over $10k, while payroll systems want to see SSN and DOB if a user changes their direct deposit. Our system knows if one of these instances occurred, and thus can grant proper permission. Conversely, if one of these customers requested user information for accounts that did not fit these descriptions, we would know there is actually a malicious actor internally (knowingly or unknowingly), and Footprint can simply reject the request.

Footprint’s vaulting system is designed to support least privilege access from the start. With Footprint, you can restrict  sub-components in your applications to only have access to certain user PII attributes (like last 4 of SSN).

aml-inquiry.title: We have an AML inquiry from our bank. How do we get the data if you store it?
content: If your bank has an AML inquiry, we make it easy to access the PII data required. As mentioned in the first Q&A, the token with the audit trail is always available to be pulled, and all other data can be easily requested by you or your banking partner. Behind the scenes, we pull the data by XX

hero.title: Why Footprint is the best KYC solution for your business
hero.subtitle: Spoiler alert: it's frictionless, secure and easy to integrate.

my-footprint.alt: With my.onefootprint.com, you can manage and see how is accessing your information

compare.introduction: In 2015, VGS ushered in the idea of tokenizing PII data and Alloy introduced a complex mechanism to handle KYC requests. Both wanted to help a new era of financial companies more easily onboard customers and store data. Footprint was founded seven years later to leverage brand-new technology to these early ideas into a more elegant, secure, and frictionless all-in-one solution that provides best-in-class KYC and PII vaulting.

rest.title: Footprint vs. the Rest
content: First let us say that we have a lot of respect for the companies that came before us, for ushering in a better age of fintech + data security. They are certainly much better than the options companies had a decade ago. However, we wouldn’t be here if we didn’t see a massive opportunity for improvement.

In the table above, we are not trying to cherry pick niche categories. Nor are we making a long table of 30 categories, most of which you don’t care about. Most importantly, we are not selling some black-box solution wrapped in unexplainable AI. Footprint is built to solve two problems, KYC + PII Vaulting, in an easy-to-use solution that does not make you choose between friction and accuracy.

It seemed silly to us that both of these functions were previously performed statically, in a moment-in-time. Not only does this make companies (and users) waste time and money going through the same process, but it lowers accuracy by not leaning on shared knowledge. Another company marking an actor as fraudulent would save other companies dollars by sharing that information in an ecosystem for KYC. Conversely, given that every company is storing essentially the same six attributes for KYC, it seems extremely unnecessary for each company to build out their own vault to store this universal information.

With Footprint, in six lines of code, you get KYC and PII Vaulting taken care of in one solution that will be running in a day.  However, we are not simply better because we do both. We believe we have a better KYC experience than companies such as Persona and Alloy, and a better PII Vaulting service than competitors like VGS.

persona.title: Footprint vs. Persona (KYC)
content: Footprint keeps the great APIs, UX + liveness of Persona, but improves it by shifting what is customized, using more novel biometrics, and decreasing overall friction.

Instead of customizing which KYC attributes are pulled and when (ie. take a SSN and then scan a driver’s license), we remove abstraction by giving a standard KYC flow with top accuracy to all customers. What we do, however, provide customization for is the other questions that are most vital to your core product. For example, many investment apps want to know someone’s risk tolerance, their investment time horizon, and amount of capital to deploy.

Persona got (deserved) praise for ushering in an age of selfie checks as an entry form of liveness. However, as fraud continues to increase, it is clear that spoofing here has been able to evade these checks and the friction for our customers is high. Instead of using faulty and cumbersome selfie checks, we leverage the biometric technology your users already have in their pockets — like FaceID.

Our one-click future will be the biggest way to decrease friction. Pardon the analogy, but we want to give people a passport to the internet, and let companies accept this standard document at passport control. Persona seemingly wants to create really great embassies to let people apply for a new passport for each account they create

alloy.title: Footprint vs. Alloy (KYC)
content: Footprint is designed to be an easier dev experience to integrate, and requires less attention from your PMs to maintain.

Alloy has become quite complex to use over the years—they now give you a buffet of vendors to chose from (82 + counting!),and provide deployed engineers to help you integrate their product. Our take is that you don’t need 82 sources to solve KYC. At the end of the day, you want to match SSNs to government data from the SSA, and you want to verify someone’s phone number by them actually using the phone. We work with fewer sources to remove unnecessary layers (call us crazy, but we think the government gives the same SSN to all of their partners), and don’t charge you for letting us “make” the decision each time of which vendor to call upon.

Alloy’s incentives are misaligned with yours. On the highest level, they want to help reduce fraud. But what happens when there is an attack. You naturally pay for more vendors. Which is exactly what Alloy wants. They have more partnerships than anyone else, and they get a nice cut each time you add one. The shame is that if one company with Alloy cut off a bad actor, it is not in Alloy’s interest to alert other companies. Because if fraud happens, those companies will pay for more vendors through Alloy!

At Footprint, we want to prevent fraud before it gets in the door. We don’t spin up a fancy risk engine very time; we actually verify good actors from the start, and flag bad actors in real-time across our ecosystem. We don’t resell any products, so we don’t have any upside in having you buy some premium experience. We just give you the most accurate results every time. Don’t get us wrong—our tech can’t do anything. We love partnering where it makes sense (see our partnerships page for ways we work with other great companies like Stytch to provide even better experiences). But we want to make KYC simple, not complex. Our goal is to save you money and reduce fraud—that is how we win and our business model.

vgs.title: Footprint vs. VGS (PII Vaulting)
content: Footprint abstracts away the complexities of storing and managing sensitive data using our cutting edge encryption infrastructure based on secure enclaves.

With classic vaulting services like VGS, you have to carefully architect your data model around their APIs: you need to choose what fields to tokenize, how they should tokenized, and where the tokenization happens (on the client side or remotely on your server). In some cases, this customization is helpful— especially if you’re building non-standard workflows—however KYC should be straightforward. Footprint combines identity verification with PII vaulting so you don’t need to glue two disparate pieces of software together. We abstract away all this complexity.

In one embedded front-end flow, Footprint collects user data direct, verifies it, performs a liveness check and then securely encrypts and tokenizes this entire user identity into a single token that binds to the audit trail of how we verified it. To integrate Footprint, you simply need to store a single user token — the “footprint user token” — which gives you granular access rights to specified identity attributes of that user.

Our simple API supports a variety of scopes and controls to maintain zero trust, least privilege principles. Generate credentials that have access to specific data attributes — or functions of those data attributes (like someone’s age versus their birthdate). All of this is invisible to your code or data model, and all you need to worry about is a single user footprint token that can be stored easily in plain-text.
